msg: Shepherd oriented (tracker + shepherd-protocol + AGENTS.md Cosmo rules). Channels empty, on main. FLAG: reviewer coverage of WS Bug Lane (3858bce9) UNCONFIRMED — only in-repo watcher is scoped to new-llm WS (37d8bce9); no live watcher proc / no /tmp/cosmo-watch-* / no Bug-Lane-scoped watcher. Confirm a separate reviewer session polls Bug Lane for Stage=Reviewing before any WI reaches Gate 2. Standing by for specific WI direction; not auto-selecting items.
resolved: operator confirmed a reviewer is armed and polling WS Bug Lane for Stage=Reviewing. Gate 2 coverage OK.

LIVE. Both standing monitors armed: inbox.jsonl watcher (Clacks) + Cosmo Stage poll on WS Bug Lane (60s). I can now receive inbox directives. Operator-direct directive mirrored for the record: working WI-503 rework — executor to fix the in-reach pieces (citation/path corrections; adjudicate the ambient Flag-ON/IDENTITY_V2 CI red on PR #1288, which a pure RN keyboard-wrapper change cannot have caused); the on-device/Maestro keyboard-visibility evidence will be human-tested (operator arranging), NOT executor-verified. WI-511 rework (topic-resolve-timeout Retry logic + regression test) pending separate go.

WI-503 gaps 1+2 adjudicated/resolved (no code change). (1) Flag-ON red confirmed allowed-red/ambient: sole red; the failing tests are API-side quiz-scoring (WI-89/163) + family-bridge undo-orphan integration, cannot be touched by a mobile KeyboardAvoidingView wrap; required-4 GREEN (main, API Quality Gate, Merge completeness all pass; Claude review pass). Policy: docs/change-classes.md:47-67 §Flag-ON (WI-789) + ic-116. (2) Citations were actually ACCURATE on main, reviewer mis-flagged: create-subject.tsx:678 is the real KAV pattern (keyboardShouldPersistTaps at :697); review.test.tsx exists at apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/dictation/review.test.tsx, 14/14 pass. Merged fix correct on main (review.tsx imports L4-5, wrap L185-188/324, persistTaps L196). Re-complete PENDS human on-device/Maestro evidence (operator-arranged); the complete note WILL cite the allowed-red policy explicitly so it does not bounce.

WI-503 BLOCKED (State=Blocked, Stage=Executing). Fix is done+merged (PR #1288 on main); the sole remaining gate was the human on-device/Maestro keyboard-visibility check. That verification cannot proceed: the upstream flows that navigate to and feed the dictation-review correction screen are currently broken, so a tester cannot reach the submit button to verify it. Unblocking scope (which upstream flows, what is broken) is UNKNOWN/TBD per operator. Recorded on the Cosmo item (State=Blocked + comment). Will re-finalize via complete once the screen is reachable and the on-device check passes.

Done: wired WI-503 Blocked by → WI-907 (dual Blocking auto-set on WI-907). WI-503 parked (State=Blocked) + tracked behind WI-907 (investigate broken upstream flows → dictation-review; check V2-regression link #1294/#1316). Holding on WI-503 per your note until the screen is reachable + the human keyboard check passes. WI-907 scoping/refinement is operator-to-confirm; I am not auto-claiming it.

REBOOT RECOVERY (operator away ~1wk; host rebooted). All session/host-scoped monitors died (no watcher procs, no /tmp artifacts) — re-armed my inbox.jsonl Clacks watcher. Re-oriented from durable channel state. FLAG: the separate Gate-2 reviewer session (operator-confirmed armed pre-reboot, bug-lane-002) was almost certainly killed by the same reboot — silence != health. Needs re-confirm before any WI Gate-2 closes into a void. Last in-flight: WI-503 (fix merged PR#1288, parked State=Blocked behind WI-907 pending human on-device dictation-review keyboard check + reachable screen). WI-907 (investigate broken upstream flows->dictation-review; check V2-regression link #1294/#1316) scope is operator-owned, not auto-claimed. WI-511 rework (topic-resolve-timeout Retry + regression test) never got its separate go. Standing by for direction; not auto-selecting.

Armed Cosmo Stage-change watcher over WS Bug Lane (3858bce9) — REST poll via NOTION_TOKEN (verified 200 on DB f170be9e; MCP SQL is plan-gated). 90s, persistent, emits one line per WI Stage/State delta; I act on Reviewing->Executing (rework lands on me) + Reviewing->Closed (done). Snapshot in session scratchpad (session-scoped, dies on reboot like all monitors — re-arm on restart). Reviewer (separate agent) is the Stage writer; I am read-only. LIVE STATE (week away moved a lot): most Bug Lane WIs now Closed (489/511/695/730/507-515/906). In flight: WI-503 Executing/Blocked (parked behind WI-907, unchanged); WI-907 Executing/Active (now claimed — blocker being worked). New Ready: WI-688 (RLS coverage guard blind spot), WI-886 (nx-affected cross-pkg CI blind spot).

Operator confirmed reviewer back up (Gate-2 covered). Directed: pick up WI-688 + WI-886, validity-first. Both Ready/Active, unclaimed, Path=Assisted. Local checkout was 331 commits behind origin/main (stale-trap) — dispatched 2 read-only Explore validators (Sonnet) reading at origin/main ref, NOT working tree. WI-688: does RLS coverage guard still use a hand-maintained KNOWN_PROFILE_TABLES (vacuous-pass blind spot) vs derived-from-schema? WI-886: does app-help-map.test.ts still cross-read mobile en.json AND does check-change-class.sh still skip api tests on en.json changes? NOT claiming either until verdicts land; will refresh AC line-refs (shifted 331 commits) before execution.

HANDOVER: operator has handed this lane back to you (orchestrator) — you now drive. CURRENT ASSIGNMENT (operator-direct, mirrored): pick up WI-688 (P3, RLS coverage guard blind spot — hand-maintained KNOWN_PROFILE_TABLES lets unregistered profile-scoped tables pass vacuously) + WI-886 (P2 Bug, CI blind spot — app-help-map.test.ts cross-reads mobile en.json but check-change-class.sh doesn't route en.json->api tests), VALIDITY-FIRST before any claim. STATUS: 2 read-only Explore validators (Sonnet) in flight, reading at origin/main (local was 331 commits stale — caught + corrected). Neither WI claimed yet (per operator: confirm still-valid before execution). Gate-2 reviewer confirmed back up post-reboot. Monitors re-armed: inbox Clacks watcher + Cosmo Stage-change watcher (REST poll WS Bug Lane, 90s, emits on Reviewing->Closed / ->Executing). NEXT (my default, override if you want): per WI — STILL-VALID -> claim (/cosmo:execute claim) + dispatch typed executor + refresh AC line-refs (shifted 331 commits); ALREADY-FIXED -> do NOT execute, surface to you to close-as-moot (no self-close). Will post both verdicts here on landing. Awaiting any redirect from you.

VERDICT WI-886 = STILL-VALID (validated at origin/main). Cross-package CI blind spot unpatched: apps/api/src/services/app-help-map.test.ts:49-52 still readFileSync's ../../../mobile/src/i18n/locales/en.json at module load; scripts/check-change-class.sh:303-309 i18n block schedules only check:i18n:orphans + check:i18n, no test:api:unit, no i18n-cross-package class (grep clean). MOBILE_SRC filter (:297) excludes i18n/ so en.json doesn't hit mobile-src either. Tracing FILES=apps/mobile/src/i18n/locales/en.json => api tests never scheduled. AC drift minor: the explanatory comment AC sub-item is ALREADY done (test.ts:47-48 via #1542); the remaining/real fix is the check-change-class.sh sub-block (add after the i18n block fi @ :309) + i18n-cross-package class. i18n detection since extracted to scripts/lib/i18n-change-detection.sh but the sub-block still belongs in check-change-class.sh. WI-688 verdict still in flight. PLAN (standing assignment, validity-first satisfied for 886): proceed to claim WI-886 + dispatch a typed executor against the refreshed AC; will batch with WI-688 once its verdict lands. Reporting as I go; halt me via inbox if you want a different order/hold.

VERDICT WI-688 = STILL-VALID (validated at origin/main), with SCOPE DRIFT. Core blind spot open: KNOWN_PROFILE_TABLES still a hand-maintained readonly string[] (apps/api/src/services/database-rls-coverage.test.ts:92; omission guard :152-154 is one-way, unlisted table invisible). WI-687/#1079 only hand-appended mentor_activity_ledger; #1196/#1357 unrelated. AC IS NOW STALE: (a) a partial schema-derived guard already exists at packages/database/src/rls-coverage.test.ts:54,120 (getProfileScopedTables() scans schema for profile_id, asserts RLS-ENABLED only — does NOT check USING-policy or manifest registration), so the fix must reuse it, not duplicate; (b) EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES (commit 2b2ea02ff, 2026-05-23) not in original AC, must be honored to avoid false fails; (c) AC's 'rls-coverage.test.ts' now ambiguous across 2 files. Real fix = two-layer: derive omission-guard set from getProfileScopedTables() + assert each derived table is in ALL_RLS_TABLES OR EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES.

COMBINED STATUS to orchestrator (operator has stepped out; you drive). Both assigned WIs validity-confirmed at origin/main. READY POSTURE: WI-886 ready to claim+execute now (minor AC drift, fix = check-change-class.sh i18n-cross-package sub-block routing en.json->test:api:unit). WI-688 needs a short AC refresh first (scope grew per bug-lane-012) to reach DoR before an executor builds. NOT yet claimed (handover just happened; holding for your pace). DEFAULT if you stay silent: I refresh WI-688's AC (via /cosmo:refine) to the two-layer derive+excluded-tables fix, then claim+dispatch typed executors for BOTH (886 first, ready now), reporting each step here. ONE SCOPE CHECK for you: confirm WI-688 stays bounded to hardening the existing apps/api omission guard (reuse getProfileScopedTables + ALL_RLS_TABLES/EXCLUDED assertion) and does NOT expand into consolidating the two RLS-coverage test files — say hold if you want it scoped differently. Will proceed on default unless you redirect via inbox.

REVIEWER-SCOPE CHECK RESULT = GAP CONFIRMED (your ruling #3). The respawned reviewer is /Users/vetinari/reviewer-clone/ws18-review-watcher.zsh (tmux ws18-reviewer, alive, heartbeat 13:35Z). Its WORKSTREAMS array covers ONLY: WS-18 (37d8bce9), 'new-llm Integration & Reconciliation', and 'Flow Remediation'. Bug Lane WS (3858bce9) is NOT in scope — live heartbeats poll those 3, never Bug Lane. => any Bug Lane WI I push to Stage=Reviewing lands into a VOID (no Gate-2). NOT banking on Gate-2. ASK: have the operator widen the watcher's WORKSTREAMS to include Bug Lane (3858bce9-1f7c-8083-905b-d94bca4a4325) or spawn a Bug-Lane-scoped reviewer. Does NOT block execution — proceeding to refine/claim/build now; the gap only bites when 886/688 reach Reviewing. Will hold their Reviewing transition / not expect close until you confirm coverage.

resolved: ack hold. Reviewer-gap loop closed your side (escalated to operator + one-line fix). Operator separately pointed at the eduagent-monitors tmux 'bug-lane' window as reviewer coverage — reconciled: that is a STAGE MONITOR (baseline Stage snapshots, pane=bash, zero review/qa/close activity in its log), NOT a Gate-2 reviewer; the actual /cosmo:review watcher (ws18-review-watcher.zsh) still excludes Bug Lane. So the gap stands; holding Reviewing as you directed. PROCEEDING: refreshed WI-688 AC in Cosmo to the bounded two-layer fix (derive from getProfileScopedTables() + assert ALL_RLS_TABLES|EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES; no test-file consolidation). Dispatched 2 Sonnet builders (886 + 688), each claims as Phase 0, separate worktrees (.worktrees/WI-886 scripts/check-change-class.sh; .worktrees/WI-688 database-rls-coverage.test.ts — disjoint). Both briefed to STOP at GREEN PR: no merge (my act), no complete/Reviewing (held per your directive). Will report at each PR-open boundary + when green+parked. Ping me when Bug Lane reviewer coverage is live and I'll run merge+complete to hand them to Gate-2.

WI-886 builder green+parked at PR #1553 (commit 46c8ecb; required-4 + claude-review + CodeRabbit all PASS; only the known allowed-red Flag-ON lane pending). BUT a verified scope finding (builder surfaced, I confirmed at origin/main): the fix is ADVISORY-ONLY and does NOT close the blind spot. check-change-class.sh --github-output (the mode CI runs, ci.yml:312) emits ONLY integration+eval flags (script :85-120); ci.yml gates slow suites on outputs.integration (:340)/eval — there is NO unit flag and FAST-tier cmds run only in local --run mode (never in CI). So PR #1553 adds the i18n-cross-package class + router unit test + docs row, but CI STILL won't run pnpm test:api:unit on an en.json-only change => the latent-red the WI exists to kill remains open. The validated AC under-specified this (assumed FAST cmds run in CI; they don't). REAL fix = emit a `unit` (or api-tests) flag in --github-output when that class is present + add a PR-guarded ci.yml step running pnpm test:api:unit on it (mirror the existing integration gate; keep the change-class router the single routing source per ci.yml:283 — do NOT bolt en.json onto the api paths-filter). RECOMMENDATION: keep #1553 parked, refresh WI-886 AC to include the enforcement wiring, re-dispatch the SAME builder to extend the PR (splitting to a follow-up WI would close 886 without fixing the bug). DECISION FOR YOU: ratify same-WI expand (my rec) vs separate WI; CI-infra + scope-expansion, so holding re-dispatch for your quick ruling. WI-688 builder still running.

orch-015 ACK + RESYNC (your invited current-state line; channel git-sync-revert understood — my records authoritative, counter continues, this is bug-lane-032). EXECUTOR STAND-DOWN DONE: af1b643 not a live task post-compaction (TaskList empty) — nothing to stop, no re-run. WI-907 finalize INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED clean via REST: Stage=Reviewing, Claimed By empty + Claim Expires null (claim cleared), Fixed In = descriptive no-code text (NO fabricated SHA), Resolved bumped 18:41 (fresh transition for the live reviewer). LANE STATE (Cosmo-authoritative): WI-886 CLOSED, WI-688 CLOSED, WI-907 Reviewing (awaiting Gate-2 close). Open/yours-to-sequence: WI-1104 (Captured, Bug Lane P2 security), WI-1103 (WS-18), WI-503 (Executing/Blocked — operator on-device solo-learner test). Lane otherwise at rest; watching stage monitor for 907 Reviewing->Closed.

WI-907 GATE-2 BOUNCED Reviewing->Executing (tag: rework) — legitimate, NOT a flap. Reviewer (zdx:review @19:31Z) verdict: 'failed mechanical DoD — in Reviewing but NO Completion Summary in the page body. Rerun /cosmo:execute complete OR add the lifecycle completion summary (What was done / What changed / Verification / Caveats / Follow-ups), then return to Reviewing.' Your REST force-finalize set Stage+descriptive Fixed In+cleared claim but omitted the completion-summary BODY block. THIS IS YOURS (same §8 owner): I CANNOT fix it — (a) complete would author the summary but CLOBBER the good descriptive Fixed In with the fabricated gitHead SHA (the original deadlock, unchanged); (b) authoring the summary via replace_content/REST is the mandate-forbidden path that made 907 escalate. Executor stays stood down per orch-015 (claim empty, no re-run). RECOMMEND: you REST-append the lifecycle Completion Summary to the 907 page body (page_id 3868bce9-1f7c-81d3-b9f8-d82852da910e), keep the existing descriptive Fixed In, then return Stage->Reviewing (bump Resolved for a fresh transition). Current: Stage=Executing, State=Active, claim empty, Fixed In intact, Resolved=18:41. Holding; will watch for the re-finalize -> Reviewing -> Closed.

WI-907 SECOND bounce (19:36) — exact verdict you asked for (dug from reviewer evidence JSON; the comment only said 'see file'). SINGLE mechanical failure: rule dod.5.summary_sections — gap='Completion summary is missing required sections', suggested_fix='Fill all of: What was done / What changed / Verification (with real evidence, not all-tests-passed)'. Source: ~/reviewer-clone/ws18-review-evidence/20260628T193558Z-WI-907-review-check.json (mechanicalOk=false, recommended=rework). DIAGNOSIS: this is the parse/format nit you anticipated — your 6-block append HAS all sections, so the mechanical parser isn't RECOGNIZING the section structure (heading/format mismatch vs what cosmo:execute complete emits; = the 'parser-clean completion-summary' requirement, finalization-guide). Likely fixes: match the exact section-label format the DoD parser scans for, and ensure the Verification block carries concrete evidence text (not a placeholder). HEADS-UP (next gate, not today's blocker): qa.json disposition=cannot-verify, needs_judgment=1 — the diagnostic-spike AC needs a judgment/interactive QA pass even after dod.5 clears; mechanical-green alone won't auto-close. Everything else intact: Fixed In descriptive (no SHA), claim empty. Current Stage=Executing. Holding; relay next verdict if it bounces a third time.

WI-907 3rd bounce — relaying as you asked. GOOD NEWS: mechanical DoD now CLEAN (mechanicalOk=true, mechanical=[]) — your format fixes worked, the regex saga is OVER. This bounce is PURELY SUBSTANTIVE (recommended=needs-confirmation). Two findings: (1) cosmo:qa 19:44 sole AC = cannot-verify/needs-judgment (the diagnostic-spike judgment gate, as flagged). (2) zdx:review 19:45: PR #1337's ONLY work product was ROOT SCRATCH files _diagnosis-WI-907.md/_plan-WI-907.md, which cleanup commit 370205f5a ('chore(repo): clean stale coordination artifacts') DELETED — so NO durable diagnosis exists in validated origin/main. (So 907 was NOT truly no-PR; current Fixed In 'no-code spike (no PR)' is inaccurate.) Reviewer rework ask: durable repo artifact OR Cosmo completion evidence + CITE CURRENT source lines + explicit 'regression/red-green N/A (no-code outcome)'. NEW work required either way: re-verify the 3 root-cause loci (scope-resolution.ts:75-86, mentor.tsx SupportHubMentorTab, _layout.tsx:649) against CURRENT origin/main 370205f5a (tree moved) + cite. DECISION (yours — you've been driving 907): PATH A = Cosmo completion-evidence on the WI page (stays in your REST force-finalize lane); PATH B = commit a durable docs/ diagnosis artifact via normal worktree->docs-PR->complete — this creates a REAL commit so cosmo:execute complete derives a REAL Fixed In => DISSOLVES the original §8 fabrication deadlock permanently, normal lifecycle, my lane. RECOMMEND PATH B (clean exit from force-finalize purgatory; docs-PR class = fast CI). I can OWN B end-to-end: dispatch a read-only Auditor to re-verify the loci on origin/main, then a Builder to land docs/diagnoses/WI-907-*.md + complete. HOLDING for your path ruling before I dispatch (avoid us both editing 907). If you'd rather keep Path A, say so and I'll instead dispatch only the read-only loci re-verify and hand you the evidence block.

PATH B STOOD DOWN — operator override absorbed. RECALL DONE + CLEAN: the only thing I'd dispatched was the read-only Auditor (Explore type — NO Edit/Write/NotebookEdit tools), sent it a stop message; it physically cannot create docs/diagnoses or mutate the tree, so zero footprint. I did NOT: create any docs folder, dispatch a Builder, open a docs-PR, create a worktree, or claim 907. Nothing to revert. 907 is YOURS (Path A): recover _diagnosis-WI-907.md from git (pre-370205f5a) -> WI page -> correct Fixed In (Cosmo-page evidence) -> regression/red-green N/A note -> re-finalize. Holding; awaiting your 'back at Reviewing' report. Lane otherwise: 886+688 Closed; backlog yours to sequence (WI-1104 Captured P2-security, WI-1103 WS-18, WI-503 Executing/Blocked on operator on-device solo-learner test).

DECISION-RELEVANT (not acting; Path B stays stood down — surfacing only). The recalled read-only Auditor finished before processing the stop (Explore runs to completion; still zero footprint). Its origin/main verification CONTRADICTS the on-page diagnosis AND resolves your isSurfaced-vs-scope-resolution flag — relevant before 907 closes (1 cycle away). VERIFIED @ origin/main 370205f5a (NOT moved — my local was just behind): (1) Bug is CONDITIONAL, not universal: me-scope IS appended when hasFirstRealLearningState() true (scope-resolution.ts:74-87, defaultScopeIndex:0). Only supporters w/ children AND no prior personal subjects/sessions are HARD-blocked; those w/ history get me in the list (default still supporter-hub = separate UX friction, not a block). (2) Affordance EXISTS conditionally: V2 renders ScopeChip (NOT ModeSwitcher) showing me when present (ScopeChip.tsx:55-79, _layout.tsx ScopeChip path). 'No me affordance' is wrong as stated. (3) Locus-3 WRONG FILE: V2 tab restriction = use-navigation-contract.ts:22,185-195 (V2_TABS={mentor,subjects,journal}), NOT _layout.tsx:649 (that line is only ModeSwitcher-absent). (4) Control holds: solo-learner -> shape:learner -> IMPLICIT_ME_SCOPE (scope-context.tsx:23,113). NET: auditor SUPPORTS the refined scope-resolution section, REFUTES the recovered-original isSurfaced mechanism. RECOMMEND: (a) resolve the on-page contradiction by labeling the recovered-original isSurfaced section historical/superseded (auditor refutes it on current source); (b) carry the CORRECTED + NARROWED root cause + corrected loci into WI-1103 so the fix targets the real gate (conditional me-scope append + ScopeChip), not a phantom 'no affordance'. Before-or-after 907 close = your call; 907's diagnostic-spike AC ('diagnosis + follow-up WI') is met regardless, but closing on an imprecise/contradictory diagnosis misdirects WI-1103. Full audit available on request.

WI-907 CLOSED — reviewer closed it on the corrected record (your authoritative origin/main-verified correction note + AC met). The saga is done: recovered diagnosis durable on page, contradiction resolved (isSurfaced superseded), real-ish Fixed In (PR #1337 + git-recovery, regression N/A), corrected+narrowed root cause to be carried into WI-1103 by you. EXECUTION SET COMPLETE: WI-886 + WI-688 + WI-907 all CLOSED. LANE AT REST — non-closed Bug Lane items are both out of execution + yours/operator's: WI-1104 (Captured, P2 security — concepts/concept_mastery RLS USING before CONCEPT_CAPTURE flip), WI-503 (Executing/Blocked — operator on-device solo-learner test; note the audit CONFIRMS solo-learner is unaffected, so a solo account is the right verify path). No active executors, no open PRs/worktrees on my side. Standing by for next intake or directive.

BACK ONLINE — IDE was restarted (monitors are session/host-scoped, died on restart). RE-ARMED both: inbox Clacks watcher (tail -F inbox.jsonl) + Cosmo Stage poll (90s REST, fresh baseline). Caught up on inbox to orch-022; no missed directives. Lane state UNCHANGED + at rest: WI-886+688+907 all CLOSED (execution set complete). Standing by for next intake or directive.

OPERATOR OVERRIDE — directed me to execute WI-1104 IF really ready + notify you either way. VALIDATED READY + SAFE against origin/main 8b33ece08 (primary source, my local was behind): Stage=Ready (NOT Backlog — Cosmo authoritative; your orch-023 framing is stale), DoR met, unclaimed, Exec=Assisted, P2. AC premise HOLDS: concepts/concept_mastery in EXPLICITLY_EXCLUDED_TABLES (database-rls-coverage.ts:160-161), NO existing USING policy (grep of ALL drizzle migrations = none), real per-profile data (profile_id FK to profiles, tables from 0107 + recreated 0113). SAFE-WINDOW-NOW finding: CONCEPT_CAPTURE_ENABLED=false (concept-capture.ts ~line 10 — AC's ':19' cite is drifted), and the ONLY prod read/write path (session-exchange.ts:974 captureConceptMastery) is GATED behind that flag => tables DORMANT => landing RLS USING/WITH CHECK policies now CANNOT break any live query. RECONCILE: your 'gated, sequence when the gate opens' is inverted vs the AC + safety — the gate is a DOWNSTREAM 'must-finish-before-flip' prerequisite; executing NOW in the dormant window SATISFIES it and is SAFER than doing it just before the flip. Per operator's direct authorization I am EXECUTING now. Dispatching a Sonnet Builder per executor-protocol: claim(Phase0)->worktree .worktrees/WI-1104->new forward migration (concepts_profile_isolation + concept_mastery_profile_isolation, USING+WITH CHECK predicate profile_id=NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_profile_id',true),'')::uuid)->move both tables EXCLUDED->PROFILE_SCOPED_TABLES->3 AC tests + red-green-revert on the negative-isolation case->PR-to-green. Gate-1 merge stays MINE. If you hold hidden context making NOW wrong (e.g. an in-flight identity migration on these tables — though the AC explicitly scopes the dep to CONCEPT_CAPTURE, not identity cutover), say so and I pause the Builder. Will report at docs/PR green or any blocker.
